data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: PLTR, AMD, MAT, PINS
data.description:
Envision Research has upgraded Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to a Buy, citing its superior positioning in the agentic AI infrastructure market. Similarly, Oakoff Investments has moved Palantir Technologies (PLTR) to a Buy rating, pointing to a resilient commercial moat and favorable risk-reward balance ahead of its upcoming earnings report.

On the other hand, Seeking Profits has downgraded Mattel (MAT) to a Hold due to lackluster execution and persistent margin pressures. Pinterest (PINS) has also been downgraded to a Sell by Amrita Roy, who identifies a significant revenue growth slowdown and a lack of clear catalysts for a turnaround in the near term.

Upgrades

  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD): Upgrade to Buy by Envision Research. The analyst argues that AMD is better positioned than its primary competitors to benefit from structural shifts in AI infrastructure demand, particularly within the realm of agentic AI.

    “Whether Q1 2026 marks a turning point for INTC or not, the yield from AMD has been much stronger in recent years. As seen, AMD has reported a positive yield every year since 2018. Looking ahead, I expect AMD’s yield to remain superior as its R&D is consolidated around a single and scalable architecture.”

  • Palantir Technologies (PLTR): Upgrade Bearish to Buy by Oakoff Investments. The analyst cites Palantir’s unique ontology layer as a critical differentiator that protects its commercial business from competition while maintaining rapid sales cycles.

    “I believe management will provide several positive commentaries, and PLTR will, once again in its corporate history, report a massive Rule of 40, outshining most other tech firms out there. The risk/reward looks favorable ahead of the earnings print, so I decided to upgrade my rating to "Buy" for the first time since mid-2024.”

Downgrades

  • Mattel (MAT): Downgrade to Hold by Seeking Profits. Despite a surprise quarterly profit, the analyst remains cautious due to fading growth prospects in the toddler segment and ongoing cost inflation driven by oil prices.

    “In Q1, the company reported a surprise profit, but there were some unique drivers. I last covered Mattel in January, rating the stock a “buy,” on expectations for acceleration in 2026. That has been one of my worst calls, with shares down 26%. Given that underperformance and with updated financials, now is a good time to revisit MAT.”

  • Pinterest (PINS): Downgrade Hold to Sell by Amrita Roy. The downgrade reflects concerns over decelerating average revenue per user and a lack of visibility regarding the impact of the company’s recent AI and advertiser diversification strategies.

    “The competitive landscape is also not favorable to Pinterest, where far larger players like Meta and Google are projected to grow their ad revenues at a faster rate in FY26, by 24% and 12%, respectively, with superior margins, indicating significant market share loss and competitive moat erosion for Pinterest.”

data.title: O'Reilly Automotive jumps over 6% after Q1 beat
data.description:
O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) shares jumped over 6% during early trading hours on Thursday after the retailer and supplier of automotive aftermarket parts reported better-than-expected first-quarter results.

For the first quarter, the firm reported GAAP EPS of $0.72, beating estimates by $0.02, while revenue of $4.56B was $100M higher than market expectations.

The company also updated its full-year 2026 guidance for revenue to $18.7B to $19B versus a consensus of $18.98B and EPS guidance of $3.15 to $3.25 versus a consensus of $3.22.

The retailer and supplier of automotive aftermarket parts expects to open 225 to 235 new stores in 2026.

Brad Beckham, O’Reilly’s CEO, commented, “We are pleased to report a strong start to 2026, highlighted by an 8.1% increase in comparable store sales and a 16% increase in our first quarter diluted earnings per share. Team O’Reilly delivered comparable store sales results exceeding our expectations in both professional and DIY, with double-digit growth in our professional business and mid-single-digit growth in DIY. Our ability to drive productivity in our business and translate robust sales growth into a 14% increase in operating profit is the direct result of our team's focus on prudent expense management.”

data.title: Lilly, Novo Nordisk gain on FDA GLP-1 compounding exclusion proposal
data.description:
Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO), the two pharmas dominating the weight loss drugs market, surged Thursday morning after the US FDA proposed placing a significant restriction on the ability of pharmacies to make compounded versions of GLP-1.

The agency is considering the removal of tirzepatide, semaglutide, and liraglutide from the 503B Bulk Drugs Substances List. If a drug is on the list, pharmacies can compound the medications from bulk substances.

In addition, in many circumstances, a drug can only be compounded if it is in shortage. The trio referenced in the FDA's news release are not in short supply.

Tirzepatide is branded by Lilly as Zepbound and Mounjaro for, respectively, obesity and type 2 diabetes. Semaglutide is sold by Novo as Wegovy and Ozempic for, respectively, obesity and diabetes. Liraglutide is marketed by Novo as Saxenda and Victoza for, respectively, obesity and diabetes.

The agency noted there is no “clinical need” for the three medications to remain on the list.

"This action reflects our responsibility to protect patients and preserve the integrity of the drug approval process while continuing to provide a transparent, science-based pathway for public input," FDA Commissioner Marty Makary said in a statement.

data.title: Xerox rises after Q1 results beat estimates, reaffirming 2026 guidance
data.description:
Xerox Holdings (XRX) soared over 35% on Thursday after reporting first quarter results that beat Wall Street expectations.

Revenue was up nearly 27% to $1.85B, driven by 30% higher sales from the print and other segment. Adjusted net loss was $0.43 per share, down $0.37 Y/Y.

“This quarter’s results demonstrated tangible progress as revenue and profit trajectory improved, adjusted operating margin expanded, and we further enhanced our liquidity,” said CEO Louie Pastor, who added that Xerox was reaffirming its 2026 guidance.

Xerox continues to expect 2026 revenue above $7.5B, adjusted operating income of $450M-$500M, and free cash flow of around $250M.

data.title: Garrett Motion shares surge on earnings beat, strong outlook
data.description:
Garrett Motion (GTX) shares jumped 22% after the company reported better-than-expected first-quarter results and raised parts of its full-year guidance.

The company posted GAAP earnings of $0.49 per share, beating estimates by $0.08, while revenue rose 12% year over year to $985M, topping expectations by more than $70M. Net income increased to $95M from $62M a year earlier.

Growth was driven by strong demand across gasoline, diesel, commercial vehicle, and industrial segments, along with favorable foreign exchange impacts and higher aftermarket sales.

Garrett also boosted its 2026 outlook, now expecting revenue of $3.6B to $3.9B, compared to estimate of $3.68B, and net income of $300M to $360M. Adjusted EBIT is projected at $520M to $600M.

Management highlighted continued momentum in both turbocharging and electrification technologies, reinforcing investor confidence and driving the stock sharply higher.

data.title: Wedbush reiterates Outperform ratings on AppLovin and Unity ahead of earnings reports
data.description:
The analysts at Wedbush reiterated their Outperform ratings on AppLovin (APP) and Unity Software (U) one week before the companies release their latest quarterly financial results.

"We expect AppLovin to continue delivering on sequential revenue growth with a staggering profit margin," said Wedbush analysts in a Thursday investor report. "Last quarter, investors looking for a massive eCommerce spike were disappointed, and once again, we expect management to sound appropriately measured on the pace of eCommerce maturation. While its measured rollout has caused concern for some, we believe it underscores AppLovin's deliberate focus on perfecting before scaling, which should drive substantial growth over the next few years."

Wedbush has a $640 price target on AppLovin.

AppLovin is slated to release its first quarter 2026 results post-market on Wednesday, May 6. A consensus estimate expects adjusted earnings per share of $3.64, GAAP EPS of $3.45, and revenue of $1.78B.

Meanwhile, Unity released preliminary  first-quarter guidance on March 26, but Wedbush believes the company might have left some room for upside. Wedbush has a $30 price target on the stock.

"With the headline already known, the formal print is less about the beat and more about the quality underneath," Wedbush noted. "Specifically, we expect largely positive commentary on the pace of Vector growth ex-ironSource, early signals on Runtime Fee adoption, and the cadence/cost of the ironSource wind-down."

Unity will release its results on Thursday, May 7. The consensus estimate calls for adjusted EPS of $0.24, GAAP EPS of ($0.21), and revenue of $504.4M.

data.title: Camping World rises on margin gains, cost cuts despite revenue decline and wider loss
data.description:
Shares of Camping World Holdings (CWH) jumped about 12% in the afternoon trading on Thursday  after the RV retailer reported lower revenue and a wider net loss but stronger margins and improved cost efficiency.

First-quarter revenue fell 4.2% to $1.35 billion, while adjusted EBITDA came in at $28 million. SG&A efficiency improved, and management highlighted better sales momentum in March and April.

The company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 outlook for adjusted EBITDA at a midpoint of about $300M, supporting investor focus on cost discipline and margin stability.

data.title: Willis Towers Watson stock slumps after disappointing Q1 revenue
data.description:
Willis Towers Watson (WTW) stock sank 14% in Thursday premarket trading, making it the biggest decliner in the S&P 500, after the insurance broking and advisory company delivered Q1 revenue that fell short of the Wall Street consensus.

In the company's 2026 outlook, it expects the Willis Re joint venture to represent a ~$0.30 headwind to adjusted EPS and its Newfront acquisition to be ~$0.10 dilutive to 2026 adjusted EPS. On the positive side, it expects a foreign currency tailwind of ~$0.10 on adjusted EPS for the rest of 2026, resulting in a ~$0.35 tailwind for the full year at today's rates.

For its long-term outlook, WTW (WTW) targets organic revenue growth in mid-single digits, with risk & broking growth in mid-single to high-single digits and health, wealth & career growth in mid-single digits.

Q1 revenue of $2.41B, just missing the $2.42B  consensus, climbed from $2.22B in Q1 2025.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $3.72, beating the average analyst estimate of $3.65, increased from $3.13 in the year-ago quarter.

Total costs of providing services grew to $1.96B from $1.79B a year ago.

Operating margin narrowed to 18.6% from 19.4% in the year-ago period.

Adjusted EBITDA of $589M, slightly exceeding the $588M consensus, climbed from $532M a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA  margin of 24.4% improved from 23.9% in last year's Q1.

Free cash flow of -$65M, vs. -$57M consensus, improved from -$86M a year ago.

data.title: Vistance Networks jumps 22% after Q1 beat, announces $1.85B RUCKUS Sale
data.description:
Vistance Networks (VISN) shares are up about 21.8% during early trading hours on Thursday after the company reported better-than-expected first quarter results.

For the first quarter, the firm reported non-GAAP EPS of $0.34, beating estimates by 0.12, while revenue of $471.8M was $9.95M higher than market estimates. Its non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA is $87.3M for the quarter. 

In other news of note, Vistance Networks (VISN) said it was selling its RUCKUS Networks unit to Belden (BDC) for $1.846B in cash, sharpening focus on its remaining business.

The firm expects net proceeds of about $1.7B after taxes and expenses and said it plans to return a significant portion to shareholders via a special distribution within 60 days of closing.

CEO Chuck Treadway said the deal “unlocks additional equity value” and enables the company to focus on its Aurora Networks business while maintaining flexibility for future investments.

data.title: Novocure climbs after late-stage win for lung cancer therapy
data.description:
Novocure (NVCR) shares surged ~31% premarket Wednesday after the oncology company said that a Phase 3 trial for its cancer therapy Tumor Treating Fields (TTFields) reached its primary endpoint in patients with brain metastases resulting from lung cancer. Its Chinese partner for TTFields, Zai Lab (ZLAB), traded flat after the announcement.

Citing topline data, Jersey-based Novocure (NVCR) said that the companies’ Phase 3 METIS trial met the primary endpoint with a statistically significant improvement in time to intracranial progression for those treated with TTFields therapy plus supportive care.

According to the company, patients on TTFields therapy plus supportive care indicated a median of 21.9 months to show intracranial progression compared to 11.3 months in those on supportive care alone.

METIS enrolled nearly 300 adults with brain metastases from non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) following radiosurgery. As for safety, Novocure (NVCR) said the treatment was well tolerated in line with previous findings.

However, at 16 weeks of median TTFields therapy, the study did not demonstrate statistical significance for key secondary endpoints, including overall survival. The company continues to analyze data to measure the effects on secondary endpoints.

Novocure (NVCR) plans to share the findings with regulators and seek a publication in a peer-reviewed medical journal.
